title: Faith
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and systematic treatment of faith on the Buddhist path, covering the three constituents of consciousness (cognitive, affective, volitional), the crucial distinction between faith and belief, practical examples from daily life, the relationship between faith and insight, obstacles to faith, and how to cultivate it. The talk has excellent literary qualities with vivid analogies (mountain climbing, relationships, doctors), personal anecdotes, and a clear pedagogical structure that would serve well as a chapter spine.
summary: A thorough exploration of faith (saddha) as a spiritual faculty, distinguishing it from belief and examining its cognitive, emotional, and volitional dimensions. The talk covers how faith develops through hearing, reflection, and practice, what undermines it, and how it becomes unshakable upon stream-entry.

relevance.note: Entirely dedicated to exploring faith as spiritual faculty
